title: selinum lachenalii
intro: Selinum lachenalii, also known as Lachenal's burnet, is a biennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactones and coumarins. The health benefits of Selinum lachenalii include its potential to aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. In herbal preparations, Selinum lachenalii is often used in tinctures, infusions, and salves to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
